unit.note: scripts/*.test.mjs structural suite via `node --test` (10 new for the .sh + 35 existing .ps1/helpers); server/client vitest not re-run — this diff has zero TypeScript delta (shell scripts + docs + .gitattributes only).

surface_verification.justification: Operational deploy tooling: bash twins (scripts/start-server-production.sh + stop-server.sh) of the Windows production rebuild+restart .ps1, plus doc (guide.md, README.md) and .gitattributes edits. No web/UI surface and ZERO TypeScript delta — no API route, store mutation, SSE/WS handler, or message contract touched — so the Backend-affects-Frontend rule does not trigger. The scripts' end-to-end deploy behaviour cannot be safely exercised here: no macOS/Linux host is available, and running the script on this Windows dev box would rebuild+kill the live production server (disallowed). This matches the established structural-test-only bar for the parallel start-server-production.ps1. Verification = start-server-production.sh.test.mjs (10 assertions pinning the build-before-kill + double ~/.claude.json-heal contracts), `bash -n` syntax check on both scripts, and confirmation that repair-claude-json.mjs is cross-platform (os.homedir()/path.join). scripts/*.test.mjs 45/45 green.

behaviors.behavior: start-server-production.sh installs+builds BOTH halves before killing the old server (a failed install/build leaves the running server untouched — ORDER MATTERS contract)
behaviors.evidence: start-server-production.sh.test.mjs: "every npm install runs BEFORE the server-kill" + "every npm run build runs BEFORE the server-kill" + "the FIRST npm install precedes the FIRST build" — all PASSED (blocks precede the "Stopping the old server" marker).

behaviors.behavior: start-server-production.sh heals ~/.claude.json TWICE — Step 0 (before the build) and Step 5 (after the server-kill + server-up confirmation)
behaviors.evidence: start-server-production.sh.test.mjs: ">= 2 repair invocations", "FIRST repair before the build", "LAST repair after the server-kill", "LAST repair after the server-up confirmation" — all PASSED.

behaviors.behavior: Both new scripts are syntactically valid bash
*.sh files are pinned to LF (eol=lf) so a Windows-authored script cannot ship CRLF (which fails on macOS with "bad interpreter: env bash^M")
behaviors.evidence: `git ls-files --eol` shows i/lf + attr/text eol=lf for all three .sh; `git cat-file blob` reports CR=0 for each staged blob.

behaviors.behavior: The ~/.claude.json self-heal helper invoked by the .sh is cross-platform (resolves the home dir portably, no Windows-only paths)
behaviors.evidence: Audit of scripts/repair-claude-json.mjs: targets `path.join(os.homedir(), ".claude.json")` and uses path.join throughout — no %USERPROFILE% / drive-letter / win32-only branch on the target path.

behaviors.behavior: start-server-production.sh actually stops the old server (lsof/kill of the :PORT listener + tsx pids), backgrounds the fresh build via nohup, and confirms the port is bound
behaviors.evidence: Requires a real macOS/Linux host with a live server process to start/stop; not available here and destructive on the Windows dev box (would kill the running production server). Same structural-test-only bar as the parallel start-server-production.ps1, which likewise has no end-to-end test.

behaviors.behavior: stop-server.sh kills the :PORT listener + tsx process, or reports "nothing was running"
behaviors.evidence: Requires real listener/tsx processes to kill on a macOS/Linux host; destructive to exercise here. Parity with stop-server.ps1, which also ships without a test. Syntax verified via `bash -n`.
